message:
perf(buffer): add set_fast inline fast-path and optimize dirty-span + fill

Optimize the three hottest paths in the render buffer:

buffer.rs - set_fast() (new, +36 lines):
  Inline fast-path for the overwhelmingly common single-width, non-
  continuation cell write. Bypasses scissor intersection, opacity
  blending, and wide-cell overlap cleanup when the trivial conditions
  hold (base scissor stack, base opacity stack, target cell is also
  single-width). Falls through to full set() for any non-trivial case,
  so correctness is always preserved.

buffer.rs - mark_dirty_span() optimization:
  Replace per-cell loop with slice::fill(1) and a pre-counted
  iter().filter() for newly-dirty cells. Eliminates branch-per-pixel
  overhead and lets the compiler vectorize the memset.

buffer.rs - fill() medium-path (+26 lines):
  Add a direct-write path for partial-width fills with opaque single-
  width cells under the base scissor/opacity. Skips per-cell overlap
  cleanup since fill overwrites the entire clipped region, and marks
  dirty spans per-row instead of per-cell.

buffer_bench.rs:
  Add set_fast_single benchmark (single cell inline path) and
  fill_half_width_200x60 benchmark (medium-path partial fill on large
  buffer) to track regression in these new paths.

ftui-layout/Cargo.toml:
  Add rustc-hash 2.1 dependency for faster hashing in layout internals.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
